nativeRange.habitat: Seasonally flooded wetlands, marsh edges, slow shallow rivers, and pond margins across tropical lowland South America

decorPreferences: open foreground or midground for runners

summary: The Chain Sword Narrow Leaf (Helanthium bolivianum) is a grass-like rosette plant from South America, widely regarded as one of the more beginner-friendly aquarium plants. It spreads readily by runners and is commonly used to form a low carpet of narrow green blades in the foreground or midground.
careNotes:
NOMENCLATURE NOTE: The older trade names Echinodorus quadricostatus and Echinodorus quadricostas are now treated as synonyms of Helanthium bolivianum, though both names remain common on product pages and in hobbyist discussions. This species is closely related to and frequently confused with Helanthium tenellum (the Pygmy Chain Sword); the two look similar and share comparable care requirements, but H. bolivianum generally grows somewhat larger. A separate entry covers [helanthium-tenellum].

Blade length in this species is notably flexible. Under bright light and as a foreground carpet, mature blades are commonly reported in the 5–10 cm range. In lower light conditions or when used as a midground accent, the plant tends to stretch to 15–20 cm.

The Chain Sword Narrow Leaf is generally considered one of the easier aquarium plants to keep. It tolerates a fairly wide temperature range — 20–28 °C is the comfortable band, with broader tolerances reported in its natural habitat — along with a pH of around 5.5–7.5 and soft to moderately hard water. CO2 injection is optional but tends to produce denser, more compact growth; a target of around 10–40 mg/l is commonly cited where supplementation is used. A nutrient-rich substrate or regular root tabs tend to yield the best results, and a balanced all-in-one liquid fertilizer is generally recommended to support the plant's fast growth rate.
breedingNotes: Propagates readily by sending out runners (stolons) that produce daughter rosettes along the substrate, forming the chain that gives the plant its common name. Has been reported to flower when grown emersed.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Trade-named product page sold as 'Echinodorus quadricostas'. Care prose on the page itself references E. tenellus; this conflation is common in the trade. Used for placement, lighting, growth-rate, substrate, and propagation guidance.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (temp 20-26 C optimal / 10-30 C tolerated, pH 5-7.5, kH 0-14 dKH), CO2 (10-40 mg/l), height (under 5 cm short form, over 20 cm tall form), growth rate (fast), propagation (runners), and foreground-to-midground placement. Authoritative species-level coverage of H. bolivianum/quadricostatus.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Alismataceae) and synonymy: Echinodorus quadricostatus Fassett and Echinodorus bolivianus (Rusby) Holm-Niels are both junior synonyms of Helanthium bolivianum. Native range stated as southern Mexico, Central America, the West Indies, and South America.
